a_image.alt: Retro Futurism style mood board — Design Movement · 1950s–1970s

sections.a_description: How the past imagined the future — starburst shapes radiating from polished chrome, atomic-age optimism, orbiting ellipses, and the streamlined elegance of jet-age design translated to the screen. Retro Futurism channels the aspirational spirit of 1950s–70s space-race culture, where every surface gleamed and the future was a place of wonder, not anxiety.

Retro Futurism appears on aerospace brand sites, science museum digital experiences, mid-century furniture shops, and creative agencies that want their identity to feel both timeless and forward-thinking. It's a go-to for podcast branding, space-themed event pages, and any project that wants to evoke optimism with a golden, starlit palette.

a_image_cover.alt: Retro Futurism style use case illustration
Retro Futurism style use case illustration
Retro Futurism style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The optimistic future-past — how the 1950s–70s imagined the year 2000. Deep midnight blue (#0b1026) background with a starburst radial gradient in warm gold (#ffd54f) emanating from the upper-right corner at low opacity, evoking a sunrise over a space colony. A pseudo-element adds concentric elliptical rings (thin gold borders) suggesting planetary orbits. The .inner panel has a subtle 1px gold border and no background — letting the starburst shine through. Text is crisp white (#f5f5f5) with gold (#ffd54f) accents on the eyebrow and heading. Buttons are streamlined with slight border-radius (6px) — not fully rounded, channeling jet-age aerodynamic styling. Set in Space Grotesk for headings — a proportional monospace-inspired sans-serif whose geometric skeleton and retro-technical feel evoke mid-century space program typography. Body in DM Sans — a clean geometric sans-serif with a neutral, modern sensibility that keeps the body readable. Heading at weight 700, 32px, letter-spacing -0.5px, line-height 1.2, gold color. Eyebrow in Space Grotesk at weight 500, 12px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, gold. Body in DM Sans at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7. Buttons in Space Grotesk, weight 600, uppercase, 1px letter-spacing. Primary: gold background (#ffd54f), dark text (#0b1026). Secondary: transparent with gold border. Hover adds glow and lift. Alternatives: Outfit (geometric humanist), Urbanist (geometric neo-grotesque), Syne (wide geometric with character).
